channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Israel says it struck Hezbollah weapons smuggling sites in Syria, testing a fragile ceasefire
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israeli aircraft struck Hezbollah weapons smuggling sites along Syria's border with Lebanon, the Israeli military said Saturday, testing a fragile, days-old ceasefire that halted months of fighting between the sides but has seen continued sporadic fire.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, BASSEM MROUE and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump tariffs threaten to crack open North American economies
item.description: MEXICO CITY — President-elect Trump’s pledge to impose tariffs on Mexico and Canada threatens to upend decades of North American integration, a bumpy process that’s recast the two U.S. neighbors as providers of raw and finished goods for the world’s biggest economy.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, The Hill

item.title: Taiwan's president departs for Pacific visit with a two-day stop in the US. China isn't happy
item.description: Taiwan’s president departed for his trip to the South Pacific that will include a two-day transit in the U.S., his first since assuming office, though few details about his itinerary are public.
item.dc:creator: Taijing Wu And Huizhong Wu, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Iceland votes for a new parliament amid disagreements on immigration, energy policy and the economy
item.description: Icelanders are electing a new parliament after disagreements over immigration, energy policy and the economy forced Prime Minister Bjarni Benediktsson to pull the plug on his coalition government and call early elections.
item.dc:creator: Marco Di Marco And Danica Kirka, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Imprisoned Kremlin critic convicted again, receives 3-year sentence for opposing war in Ukraine
item.description: Imprisoned Kremlin critic Alexei Gorinov was convicted again on Friday and sentenced to three years in prison for opposing Russia's ongoing war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fishers at a Lebanese port hope ceasefire with Israel means normal life is returning
item.description: The ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah brought hope for normality back to many in southern Lebanon on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Bassem Mroue, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Romania's parliamentary vote risks being overshadowed by presidential race chaos
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — Hundreds of protesters gathered in Bucharest after a far-right populist unexpectedly won the first round of a presidential race, plunging Romania into turmoil just days before the country holds parliamentary elections. “Better to be dead than a fascist,” one placard reads.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN McGRATH, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: What to know about sudden rebel gains in Syria's 13-year war and why it matters
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The 13-year civil war in Syria has roared back into prominence with a surprise rebel offensive on Aleppo, one of Syria's largest cities and an ancient business hub.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump wants a Gaza ceasefire deal 'now,' Graham says
item.description: Sen. Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.) said that President-elect Trump wants to reach a ceasefire and hostage deal before inauguration day on Jan. 20, Axios reported on Friday. "Trump is more determined than ever to release the hostages and supports a ceasefire that includes a hostage deal.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: What to know about shock offensive on Syria's largest city
item.description: Syrian rebels against the country's President Bashar Al-Assad advanced toward one of the largest cities in the country — the first time since 2016 — following a shock offensive launched by insurgents on Wednesday, the Associated Press reported on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Ireland headed for coalition government following parliamentary election, exit poll suggests
item.description: DUBLIN (AP) — An exit poll in Ireland’s parliamentary election released late Friday suggests the three biggest parties have won roughly equal shares and the country is headed for another coalition government. A poll released as voting ended at 10 p.m.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS and PETER MORRISON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canadian news publishers sue OpenAI over alleged copyright infringement
item.description: OTTAWA, Ontario (AP) — A coalition of Canadian news publishers, including The Canadian Press, Torstar, Globe and Mail, Postmedia and CBC/Radio-Canada, has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI for using news content to train its ChatGPT generative artificial intelligence system.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: UK lawmakers vote in favor of bill legalizing assisted dying
item.description: Lawmakers in Britain gave initial approval to a bill to allow terminally ill adults to end their lives in England and Wales in a vote in the House of Commons on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democrats sound alarm on West Bank under Trump administration
item.description: House Democrats are sounding early alarms that a second Trump administration will lead to a vast erosion of Palestinian rights and undermine efforts to bring peace to the volatile Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, The Hill

item.title: Israelis are wary of returning to the north because they don't trust the ceasefire with Hezbollah
item.description: KIBBUTZ MALKIYA, Israel (AP) — Dean Sweetland casts his gaze over a forlorn street in the Israeli community of Kibbutz Malkiya. Perched on a hill overlooking the border with Lebanon, the town stands mostly empty after being abandoned a year ago. The daycare is closed. The homes are unkempt.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UK Cabinet member resigns over past phone fraud case
item.description: A member of the U.K. Cabinet has resigned after reports revealed she was convicted in a fraud case more than a decade ago.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Amazon workers in India join Black Friday strike action for better wages and working conditions
item.description: NEW DELHI (AP) — Amazon staff in India have joined strike action calling for better wages and working conditions as the company prepares for one of the busiest shopping periods of the year.
item.dc:creator: SHONAL GANGULY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UK spy chief says Russia waging 'reckless' sabotage campaign in Europe
item.description: The head of Britain's Secret Intelligence Service said Russia's war against Ukraine is part of a “staggeringly reckless campaign of Russian sabotage” aimed at Europe.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Putin praises 'intelligent' Trump amid assassination threats
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin praised President-elect Trump as an “intelligent” and “experienced” leader Thursday, while noting he does not believe the president-elect is “safe” following the two attempts on his life earlier this year.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Trump shake-up spurs optimism and anxiety among key US allies
item.description: Some of America's key security allies are hopeful President-elect Trump's impetuous streak might spur resolution to drawn-out conflicts in Ukraine and the Middle East, but there's also deep anxiety about the “America First” agenda, compounded this week by Trump's tariff threats.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Negotiators get closer to agreeing on a plastic pollution treaty
item.description: BUSAN, South Korea (AP) — Negotiators working on a treaty to address the global crisis of plastic pollution inched closer to an agreement Friday, with more countries saying they want to address the total plastic on Earth.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russian defense minister visits North Korea for talks with military and political leaders
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — Russian Defense Minister Andrei Belousov arrived in North Korea on Friday for talks with North Korean military and political leaders as the countries deepen their cooperation over Russia’s war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Missing Canadian hiker found alive after spending weeks in blistering cold
item.description: A missing hiker was located in the Canadian wilderness after being lost for more than six weeks, according to the Royal Canadian Mounted Police.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Missing hiker survived 50 days in Canadian wilderness, at times in frigid temps
item.description: A 20-year-old hiker who had been missing for weeks was found alive in the wilderness of British Columbia, the Royal Canadian Mountain Police confirmed on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Unger, NewsNation

item.title: Putin mulls striking Kyiv with new hypersonic missile that can reportedly reach US West Coast
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin says that Ukraine’s "decision-making centers" in Kyiv could be targeted next using a new hypersonic missile that could also potentially reach the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia launches another large missile, drone attack on Ukraine's energy infrastructure
item.description: Russia launched another attack on Ukraine's energy infrastructure on Thursday, leaving over a million households without power and forcing emergency power to be implemented nationwide.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 2,000-year-old fig unearthed in Ireland marks ‘oldest example of an exotic fruit’ discovered in the area
item.description: During excavations in Ireland, an ancient fig dating back 2,000 years was unearthed. The finding of the fig marks a fist-of-its kind discovery for Ireland.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China releases 3 Americans in prisoner swap
item.description: Mark Swidan, Kai Li and John Leung were freed after years of talks between U.S. and Chinese officials.
item.dc:creator: Katie Smith, NewsNation

item.title: China releases 3 'wrongfully detained' Americans, White House says
item.description: China is releasing three Americans who were "wrongfully detained," a National Security Council spokesperson says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Amelia Earhart’s plane photo not of the plane, company says
item.description: “After 11 months the waiting has finally ended and unfortunately our target was not Amelia's Electra 10E," the exploration company said.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, NewsNation

item.title: Bah, humbug! Vandal smashes Ebenezer Scrooge's tombstone used in 'A Christmas Carol' movie
item.description: If life imitates art, a vandal may be haunted by The Ghost of Christmas Yet to Come for smashing a tombstone that marked the fictional grave of Ebenezer Scrooge in the English countryside.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Drones detected near 3 US bases in England last week: Air Force
item.description: Between Wednesday and Friday, multiple unidentified drones were detected near RAF Lakenheath, RAF Mildenhall and RAF Feltwell.
item.dc:creator: Elina Shirazi, NewsNation
